\"http://example.com/foo\", nil)\n\t\tw   = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t)\n\n\tcfg.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n\tresp := w.Result()\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\n\tassert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, resp.StatusCode)\n\tassert.NotEmpty(t, body)\n}\n\n// readyYAMLIntoEnv parses the file provided at path as YAML.\n// It walks the keys and values and builds up a set of environment variables\n// compatible with viper's expectations for automatic env capability.\nfunc readYAMLIntoEnv(t *testing.T, path string) [][2]string {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tconfigFile, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\tvar config map[any]any\n\terr = yaml.Unmarshal(configFile, &config)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\treturn getEnvVars(\"flipt\", config)\n}\n\nfunc getEnvVars(prefix string, v map[any]any) (vals [][2]string) {\nOUTER:\n\tfor key, value := range v {\n\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\t\tcase map[any]any:\n\t\t\tvals = append(vals, getEnvVars(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%v\", prefix, key), v)...)\n\t\tcase []any:\n\t\t\tbuilder := strings.Builder{}\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range v {\n\t\t\t\tswitch s := s.(type) {\n\t\t\t\tcase map[any]any:\n\t\t\t\t\t// descend into slices of objects\n\t\t\t\t\tvals = append(vals, getEnvVars(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%v\", prefix, key), s)...)\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue OUTER\n\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", s))\n\t\t\t\t\tif i < len(v)-1 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteByte(' ')\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tvals = append(vals, [2]string{\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%s\", strings.ToUpper(prefix), strings.ToUpper(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", key))),\n\t\t\t\tbuilder.String(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tvals = append(vals, [2]string{\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%s_%s\", strings.ToUpper(prefix), strings.ToUpper(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", key))),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", value),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn vals\n}\n\nfunc TestMarshalYAML(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname    string\n\t\tpath    string\n\t\twantErr error\n\t\tcfg     func() *Config\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"defaults\",\n\t\t\tpath: \"./testdata/marshal/yaml/default.yml\",\n\t\t\tcfg:  Default,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\twantErr := tt.wantErr\n\n\t\t\texpected, err := os.ReadFile(tt.path)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tout, err := yaml.Marshal(tt.cfg())\n\t\t\tif wantErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Log(err)\n\t\t\t\tmatch := false\n\t\t\t\tif errors.Is(err, wantErr) {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatch = true\n\t\t\t\t} else if err.Error() == wantErr.Error() {\n\t\t\t\t\tmatch = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\trequire.True(t, match, \"expected error %v to match: %v\", err, wantErr)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tassert.YAMLEq(t, string(expected), string(out))\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\ntype sliceEnvBinder []string\n\nfunc (s *sliceEnvBinder) MustBindEnv(v ...string) {\n\t*s = append(*s, v...)\n}\n\nfunc Test_mustBindEnv(t *testing.T) {\n\tfor _, test := range []struct {\n\t\tname string\n\t\t// inputs\n\t\tenv []string\n\t\ttyp any\n\t\t// expected outputs\n\t\tbound []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"simple struct\",\n\t\t\tenv:  []string{},\n\t\t\ttyp: struct {\n\t\t\t\tA string `mapstructure:\"a\"`\n\t\t\t\tB string `mapstructure:\"b\"`\n\t\t\t\tC string `mapstructure:\"c\"`\n\t\t\t}{},\n\t\t\tbound: []string{\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"nested structs with pointers\",\n\t\t\tenv:  []string{},\n\t\t\ttyp: struct {\n\t\t\t\tA string `mapstructure:\"a\"`\n\t\t\t\tB struct {\n\t\t\t\t\tC *struct {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tD int\n\t\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"c\"`\n\t\t\t\t\tE []string `mapstructure:\"e\"`\n\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"b\"`\n\t\t\t}{},\n\t\t\tbound: []string{\"a\", \"b.c.d\", \"b.e\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"structs []string{\"A_B_FOO\", \"A_B_BAR\", \"A_B_BAZ\"},\n\t\t\ttyp: struct {\n\t\t\t\tA struct {\n\t\t\t\t\tB map[string]string `mapstructure:\"b\"`\n\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"a\"`\n\t\t\t}{},\n\t\t\t// no {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tE int `mapstructure:\"e\"`\n\t\t\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"d\"`\n\t\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"b\"`\n\t\t\t\t} `mapstructure:\"a\"`\n\t\t\t}{},\n\t\t\t// no environment variable to {\n\tconfigType := reflect.TypeFor[Config]()\n\tconfigTags := getStructTags(configType)\n\n\tfor k, v := range camelCaseMatchers {\n\t\tstrcase.ConfigureAcronym(k, v)\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate the struct tags for the Config struct.\n\t// recursively validate the struct tags for all sub-structs.\n\tvalidateStructTags(t, configTags, configType)\n}\n\nfunc validateStructTags(t *testing.T, tags map[string]map[string]string, tType reflect.Type) {\n\ttName := tType.Name()\n\tfor fieldName, fieldTags := range tags {\n\t\tfieldType, ok := tType.FieldByName(fieldName)\n\t\trequire.True(t, ok, \"field %s not found in type %s\", fieldName, tName)\n\n\t\t// Validate the `json` struct tag.\n\t\tjsonTag, ok := fieldTags[\"json\"]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\trequire.True(t, isCamelCase(jsonTag), \"json tag for field '%s.%s' should be camelCase but is '%s'\", tName, fieldName, jsonTag)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Validate the `mapstructure` struct tag.\n\t\tmapstructureTag, ok := fieldTags[\"mapstructure\"]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\trequire.True(t, isSnakeCase(mapstructureTag), \"mapstructure tag for field '%s.%s' should be snake_case but is '%s'\", tName, fieldName, mapstructureTag)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Validate the `yaml` struct tag.\n\t\tyamlTag, ok := fieldTags[\"yaml\"]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\trequire.True(t, isSnakeCase(yamlTag), \"yaml tag for field '%s.%s' should be snake_case but is '%s'\", tName, fieldName, yamlTag)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// recursively validate the struct tags for all sub-structs.\n\t\tif fieldType.Type.Kind() == reflect.Struct {\n\t\t\tvalidateStructTags(t, getStructTags(fieldType.Type), fieldType.Type)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc isCamelCase(s string) bool {\n\treturn s == strcase.ToLowerCamel(s)\n}\n\nfunc isSnakeCase(s string) bool {\n\t// Special case: sigv4 follows the naming convention\n\tif s == \"sigv4\" {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn s == strcase.ToSnake(s)\n}\n\nfunc getStructTags(t reflect.Type) map[string]map[string]string {\n\ttags := make(map[string]map[string]string)\n\n\tfor field := range t.Fields() {\n\t\t// Get the field name.\n\t\tfieldName := field.Name\n\n\t\t// Get the field tags.\n\t\tfieldTags := make(map[string]string)\n\t\tfor _, tag := range []string{\"json\", \"mapstructure\", \"yaml\"} {\n\t\t\ttagValue := field.Tag.Get(tag)\n\t\t\tif tagValue == \"-\" {\n\t\t\t\tfieldTags[tag] = \"skip\"\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tvalues := strings.Split(tagValue, \",\")\n\t\t\tif len(values) > 1 {\n\t\t\t\ttagValue = values[0]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif tagValue != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfieldTags[tag] = tagValue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttags[fieldName] = fieldTags\n\t}\n\n\treturn tags\n}\n\nfunc TestLicenseConfig_validate(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct 218→}\n   219→\n   220→func buildConfig(ctx context.Context) (*zap.Logger, *config.Config, error) {\n   221→\tpath, found := determineConfig(providedConfigFile)\n   222→\n   223→\t// Load config   230→\t\tdefaultLogger.Info(\"no configuration file found, using defaults\")\n   231→\t}\n   232→\n   233→\tcfg := res.Config\n   234→\n   235→\tencoding := defaultEncoding\n   236→\tencoding.TimeKey 242→\t// log to file if enabled\n   243→\tif cfg.Log.File != \"\" {\n   244→\t\tloggerConfig.OutputPaths = []string{cfg.Log.File}\n   245→\t}\n   246→\n   247→\t// parse/set log level\n 259→\t\tloggerConfig.EncoderConfig.EncodeLevel = zapcore.CapitalLevelEncoder\n   260→\t}\n   261→\n   262→\tlogger := zap.Must(loggerConfig.Build())\n   263→\n   264→\t// print out any warnings from 285→func run(ctx context.Context, logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config) error {\n   286→\tvar err error\n   287→\n   288→\tshutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 299→\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"creating otel log exporter: %w\", err)\n   300→\t\t}\n   301→\n   302→\t\tdefer func() {\n   303→\t\t\t_ = logsExpShutdown(shutdownCtx)\n   304→\t\t}()\n   305→\n   306→\t\tlogProcessor := log.NewBatchProcessor(logsExp)\n   307→\t\tdefer func() {\n   308→\t\t\t_ = logProcessor.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)\n   309→\t\t}()\n   310→\n   311→\t\tloggerProvider := zap.String(\"date\", date), zap.String(\"go_version\", goVersion))\n   331→\t}\n   332→\n   333→\tvar (\n   334→\t\tisRelease   = release.Is(version)\n   335→\t\treleaseInfo release.Info\n   336→\t)\n   337→\n   338→\tif cfg.Meta.CheckForUpdates && isRelease {\n   339→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"checking for updates\")\n   340→\n   341→\t\treleaseInfo, err = release.Check(ctx, version)\n   342→\t\tif err != nil {\n   343→\t\t\tlogger.Warn(\"checking for updates\", zap.Error(err))\n   344→\t\t}\n   345→\n   346→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"version info\", zap.String(\"current_version\", releaseInfo.CurrentVersion), zap.String(\"latest_version\", releaseInfo.LatestVersion))\n   347→\n   348→\t\tif isConsole {\n   349→\t\t\tif releaseInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n   350→\t\t\t\tcolor.Yellow(\"A newer version of Flipt exists at %s, \\nplease consider updating to the latest version.\", releaseInfo.LatestVersionURL)\n   351→\t\t\t} else {\n   352→\t\t\t\tcolor.Green(\"You are currently running the latest version of Flipt [%s]!\", releaseInfo.CurrentVersion)\n   353→\t\t\t}\n   354→\t\t} else {\n   355→\t\t\tif releaseInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n   356→\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"newer version available\", zap.String(\"version\", releaseInfo.LatestVersion), zap.String(\"url\", releaseInfo.LatestVersionURL))\n   357→\t\t\t} else {\n   358→\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"running latest version\", zap.String(\"version\", releaseInfo.CurrentVersion))\n   359→\t\t\t}\n   360→\t\t}\n   361→\t}\n   362→\n   363→\t// see: https://consoledonottrack.com/\n   364→\tif isSet(dntVar) && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n   365→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"DO_NOT_TRACK environment variable set, disabling telemetry\")\n   366→\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n   367→\t}\n   368→\n   369→\tif isSet(ciVar) && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n   370→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"CI detected, disabling telemetry\")\n   371→\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n   372→\t}\n   373→\n   374→\tif !isRelease && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n   375→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"not a release version, disabling telemetry\")\n   376→\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n   377→\t}\n   378→\n   379→\tg, ctx := cfg.Meta.StateDirectory), zap.Error(err))\n   383→\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n   384→\t} else {\n   385→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"local state directory exists\", zap.String(\"path\", cfg.Meta.StateDirectory))\n   386→\t}\n   387→\n   388→\t// initialize secrets manager and resolve secrets in config before creating any components\n   389→\tsecretsManager, err := keygenProductID, &cfg.License, licenseManagerOpts...)\n   419→\n   420→\tdefer func() {\n   421→\t\t// Use a dedicated timeout context for deactivation to avoid competing with other shutdown resolveSecretsInConfig(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n   527→\treturn walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem(), secretsManager)\n   528→}\n   529→\n   530→// walkConfigForSecrets recursively walks the config struct and resolves secret references\n   531→func walkConfigForSecrets(ctx context.Context, v reflect.Value, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n   532→\tswitch v.Kind() {\n   533→\tcase reflect.String:\n   534→\t\tif v.CanSet() {\n   535→\t\t\tstr := v.String()\n   536→\t\t\t// Check if this is a secret reference using the regex\n   537→\t\t\tif secretReference.MatchString(str) {\n   538→\t\t\t\t// Extract the reference: ${secret:reference} -> reference\n   539→\t\t\t\treference := secretReference.ReplaceAllString(str, `$1`)\n   540→\n   541→\t\t\t\t// Parse the reference format: either \"key\" or \"provider:key\"\n   542→\t\t\t\tparts := strings.Split(reference, \":\")\n   543→\n   544→\t\t\t\tvar secretRef secrets.Reference\n   545→\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   546→\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 1:\n   547→\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"key-name\" - use default provider\n   548→\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n   549→\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: \"\", // Use default provider\n   550→\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[0],\n   551→\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[0],\n   552→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   553→\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 2:\n   554→\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"provider:key\"\n   555→\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n   556→\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: parts[0],\n   557→\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[1],\n   558→\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[1],\n   559→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   560→\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   561→\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid secret reference format %q, expected 'key' or 'provider:key'\", reference)\n   562→\t\t\t\t}\n   563→\n   564→\t\t\t\t// Resolve the secret\n   565→\t\t\t\tsecretValue, err := secretsManager.GetSecretValue(ctx, secretRef)\n   566→\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   567→\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resolving secret reference %q: %w\", str, err)\n   568→\t\t\t\t}\n   569→\n   570→\t\t\t\t// Replace the secret reference with the resolved value\n   571→\t\t\t\tv.SetString(string(secretValue))\n   572→\t\t\t}\n   573→\t\t}\n   574→\n   575→\tcase reflect.Struct:\n   576→\t\tfor _, field := range v.Fields() {\n   577→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, field, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   578→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   579→\t\t\t}\n   580→\t\t}\n   581→\n   582→\tcase reflect.Pointer:\n   583→\t\tif !v.IsNil() {\n   584→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Elem(), secretsManager); err != nil {\n   585→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   586→\t\t\t}\n   587→\t\t}\n   588→\n   589→\tcase reflect.Slice:\n   590→\t\tfor i := 0; i < v.Len(); i++ {\n   591→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Index(i), secretsManager); err != nil {\n   592→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   593→\t\t\t}\n   594→\t\t}\n   595→\n   596→\tcase reflect.Map:\n   597→\t\tfor _, key := range v.MapKeys() {\n   598→\t\t\tvalue := v.MapIndex(key)\n   599→\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Interface && !value.IsNil() {\n   600→\t\t\t\tvalue = value.Elem()\n   601→\t\t\t}\n   602→\n   603→\t\t\t// Map values from MapIndex are not addressable, so we need to create\n   604→\t\t\t// an addressable copy, resolve secrets in it, and set it back\n   605→\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Struct {\n   606→\t\t\t\t// Create a new addressable copy of the struct\n   607→\t\t\t\tnewValue := reflect.New(value.Type()).Elem()\n   608→\t\t\t\tnewValue.Set(value)\n   609→\n   610→\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, newValue, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   611→\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n   612→\t\t\t\t}\n   613→\n   614→\t\t\t\t// Set the modified value back into the map\n   615→\t\t\t\tv.SetMapIndex(key, newValue)\n   616→\t\t\t} else {\n   617→\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, value, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   618→\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n   619→\t\t\t\t}\n   620→\t\t\t}\n   621→\t\t}\n   622→\t}\n   623→\n   624→\treturn nil\n   625→}\n   626→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, currently running the latest version of Flipt [%s]!\", releaseInfo.CurrentVersion)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tif releaseInfo.UpdateAvailable {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"newer version available\", zap.String(\"version\", releaseInfo.LatestVersion), zap.String(\"url\", releaseInfo.LatestVersionURL))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"running latest version\", zap.String(\"version\", releaseInfo.CurrentVersion))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// see: https://consoledonottrack.com/\n\tif isSet(dntVar) && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"DO_NOT_TRACK environment variable set, disabling telemetry\")\n\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n\t}\n\n\tif isSet(ciVar) && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"CI detected, disabling telemetry\")\n\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n\t}\n\n\tif !isRelease && cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"not a release version, disabling telemetry\")\n\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n\t}\n\n\tg, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\tif err := initMetaStateDir(cfg); err != nil {\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"disabling telemetry, state directory not accessible\", zap.String(\"path\", cfg.Meta.StateDirectory), zap.Error(err))\n\t\tcfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled = false\n\t} else {\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"local state directory exists\", zap.String(\"path\", cfg.Meta.StateDirectory))\n\t}\n\n\t// initialize secrets manager and resolve secrets in config before creating any components\n\tsecretsManager, err := secrets.NewManager(logger, cfg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"initializing secrets manager: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif secretsManager != nil {\n\t\t\t_ = operations\n\t\tdeactivateCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 15*time.Second)\n\t\t_ = licenseManagerShutdown(deactivateCtx)\n\t\tcancel()\n\t}()\n\n\t// Validate license requirements for secrets providers\n\tif cfg.Secrets.Providers.Vault != nil && cfg.Secrets.Providers.Vault.Enabled {\n\t\tif licenseManager.Product() == product.OSS {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"vault secrets provider requires a paid license\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := info.New(\n\t\tinfo.WithBuild(commit, date, goVersion, version, isRelease),\n\t\tinfo.WithLatestRelease(releaseInfo),\n\t\tinfo.WithConfig(cfg),\n\t\tinfo.WithLicenseManager(licenseManager),\n\t)\n\n\tif cfg.Meta.TelemetryEnabled {\n\t\tlogger := logger.With(zap.String(\"component\", \"telemetry\"))\n\n\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\t\treporter, err := telemetry.NewReporter(*cfg, logger, analyticsKey, analyticsEndpoint, info)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Debug(\"initializing telemetry reporter\", zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdefer func() {\n\t\t\t\t_ = reporter.Shutdown()\n\t\t\t}()\n\n\t\t\treporter.Run(ctx)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// in-process client connection for grpc services\n\tipch := &inprocgrpc.Channel{}\n\n\tvar grpcOptions []cmd.GRPCServerOption\n\tif forceMigrate {\n\t\tgrpcOptions = append(grpcOptions, cmd.WithForceMigrate())\n\t}\n\tgrpcServer, err := cmd.NewGRPCServer(ctx, logger, cfg, ipch, info, licenseManager, secretsManager, grpcOptions...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// starts grpc server\n\tg.Go(grpcServer.Run)\n\n\thttpServer, err := cmd.NewHTTPServer(ctx, logger, cfg, ipch, info)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// starts REST http(s) server\n\tg.Go(httpServer.Run)\n\n\t// block until root context is cancelled\n\t// and shutdown has been signalled\n\t<-ctx.Done()\n\n\tlogger.Info(\"shutting down...\")\n\n\t_ = httpServer.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)\n\t_ = grpcServer.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)\n\n\treturn g.Wait()\n}\n\nfunc ensureDir(path string) error {\n\tfp, err := os.Stat(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist) {\n\t\t\t// directory doesnt exist, so try to create it\n\t\t\treturn os.MkdirAll(path, 0o700)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"checking directory: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif fp != nil && !fp.IsDir() {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"not a directory\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// check if state directory already exists, create it if not\nfunc initMetaStateDir(cfg *config.Config) error {\n\tif cfg.Meta.StateDirectory == \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tcfg.Meta.StateDirectory, err = config.Dir()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ensureDir(cfg.Meta.StateDirectory)\n}\n\n// resolveSecretsInConfig walks the config structure and resolves any secret references in-place\nfunc resolveSecretsInConfig(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n\treturn walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem(), secretsManager)\n}\n\n// walkConfigForSecrets recursively walks the config struct and resolves secret references\nfunc walkConfigForSecrets(ctx context.Context, v reflect.Value, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n\tswitch v.Kind() {\n\tcase reflect.String:\n\t\tif v.CanSet() {\n\t\t\tstr := v.String()\n\t\t\t// Check if this is a secret reference using the regex\n\t\t\tif secretReference.MatchString(str) {\n\t\t\t\t// Extract the reference: ${secret:reference} -> reference\n\t\t\t\treference := secretReference.ReplaceAllString(str, `$1`)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Parse the reference format: either \"key\" or \"provider:key\"\n\t\t\t\tparts := strings.Split(reference, \":\")\n\n\t\t\t\tvar secretRef secrets.Reference\n\t\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 1:\n\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"key-name\" - use default provider\n\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: \"\", // Use default provider\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[0],\n\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[0],\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 2:\n\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"provider:key\"\n\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: parts[0],\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[1],\n\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[1],\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid secret reference format %q, expected 'key' or 'provider:key'\", reference)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Resolve the secret\n\t\t\t\tsecretValue, err := secretsManager.GetSecretValue(ctx, secretRef)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resolving secret reference %q: %w\", str, err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Replace the secret reference with the resolved value\n\t\t\t\tv.SetString(string(secretValue))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase reflect.Struct:\n\t\tfor _, field := range v.Fields() {\n\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, field, secretsManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase reflect.Pointer:\n\t\tif !v.IsNil() {\n\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Elem(), secretsManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase reflect.Slice:\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < v.Len(); i++ {\n\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Index(i), secretsManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase reflect.Map:\n\t\tfor _, key := range v.MapKeys() {\n\t\t\tvalue := v.MapIndex(key)\n\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Interface && !value.IsNil() {\n\t\t\t\tvalue = value.Elem()\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Map values from MapIndex are not addressable, so we need to create\n\t\t\t// an addressable copy, resolve secrets in it, and set it back\n\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Struct {\n\t\t\t\t// Create a new addressable copy of the struct\n\t\t\t\tnewValue := reflect.New(value.Type()).Elem()\n\t\t\t\tnewValue.Set(value)\n\n\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, newValue, secretsManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Set the modified value back into the map\n\t\t\t\tv.SetMapIndex(key, newValue)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, value, secretsManager); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n","numLines":626,"startLine":1,"totalLines":626}}}

194→\t\treturn configFile, true\n   195→\t}\n   196→\n   197→\t// otherwise, check if user config file exists on filesystem\n   198→\t_, err := os.Stat(userConfigFile)\n   199→\tif err == nil {\n 218→}\n   219→\n   220→func buildConfig(ctx context.Context) (*zap.Logger, *config.Config, error) {\n   221→\tpath, found := determineConfig(providedConfigFile)\n   222→\n   223→\t// Load config 259→\t\tloggerConfig.EncoderConfig.EncodeLevel = zapcore.CapitalLevelEncoder\n   260→\t}\n   261→\n   262→\tlogger := zap.Must(loggerConfig.Build())\n   263→\n   264→\t// print out any warnings from 270→\t\tlogger.Debug(\"configuration source\", zap.String(\"path\", path))\n   271→\t}\n   272→\n   273→\treturn logger, cfg, nil\n   274→}\n   275→\n   276→const (\n   277→\tdntVar = resolveSecretsInConfig(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n   527→\treturn walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem(), secretsManager)\n   528→}\n   529→\n   530→// walkConfigForSecrets recursively walks the config struct and resolves secret references\n   531→func walkConfigForSecrets(ctx context.Context, v reflect.Value, secretsManager secrets.Manager) error {\n   532→\tswitch v.Kind() {\n   533→\tcase reflect.String:\n   534→\t\tif v.CanSet() {\n   535→\t\t\tstr := v.String()\n   536→\t\t\t// Check if this is a secret reference using the regex\n   537→\t\t\tif secretReference.MatchString(str) {\n   538→\t\t\t\t// Extract the reference: ${secret:reference} -> reference\n   539→\t\t\t\treference := secretReference.ReplaceAllString(str, `$1`)\n   540→\n   541→\t\t\t\t// Parse the reference format: either \"key\" or \"provider:key\"\n   542→\t\t\t\tparts := strings.Split(reference, \":\")\n   543→\n   544→\t\t\t\tvar secretRef secrets.Reference\n   545→\t\t\t\tswitch {\n   546→\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 1:\n   547→\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"key-name\" - use default provider\n   548→\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n   549→\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: \"\", // Use default provider\n   550→\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[0],\n   551→\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[0],\n   552→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   553→\t\t\t\tcase len(parts) == 2:\n   554→\t\t\t\t\t// Simple format: \"provider:key\"\n   555→\t\t\t\t\tsecretRef = secrets.Reference{\n   556→\t\t\t\t\t\tProvider: parts[0],\n   557→\t\t\t\t\t\tPath:     parts[1],\n   558→\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      parts[1],\n   559→\t\t\t\t\t}\n   560→\t\t\t\tdefault:\n   561→\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid secret reference format %q, expected 'key' or 'provider:key'\", reference)\n   562→\t\t\t\t}\n   563→\n   564→\t\t\t\t// Resolve the secret\n   565→\t\t\t\tsecretValue, err := secretsManager.GetSecretValue(ctx, secretRef)\n   566→\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   567→\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resolving secret reference %q: %w\", str, err)\n   568→\t\t\t\t}\n   569→\n   570→\t\t\t\t// Replace the secret reference with the resolved value\n   571→\t\t\t\tv.SetString(string(secretValue))\n   572→\t\t\t}\n   573→\t\t}\n   574→\n   575→\tcase reflect.Struct:\n   576→\t\tfor _, field := range v.Fields() {\n   577→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, field, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   578→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   579→\t\t\t}\n   580→\t\t}\n   581→\n   582→\tcase reflect.Pointer:\n   583→\t\tif !v.IsNil() {\n   584→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Elem(), secretsManager); err != nil {\n   585→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   586→\t\t\t}\n   587→\t\t}\n   588→\n   589→\tcase reflect.Slice:\n   590→\t\tfor i := 0; i < v.Len(); i++ {\n   591→\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, v.Index(i), secretsManager); err != nil {\n   592→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   593→\t\t\t}\n   594→\t\t}\n   595→\n   596→\tcase reflect.Map:\n   597→\t\tfor _, key := range v.MapKeys() {\n   598→\t\t\tvalue := v.MapIndex(key)\n   599→\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Interface && !value.IsNil() {\n   600→\t\t\t\tvalue = value.Elem()\n   601→\t\t\t}\n   602→\n   603→\t\t\t// Map values from MapIndex are not addressable, so we need to create\n   604→\t\t\t// an addressable copy, resolve secrets in it, and set it back\n   605→\t\t\tif value.Kind() == reflect.Struct {\n   606→\t\t\t\t// Create a new addressable copy of the struct\n   607→\t\t\t\tnewValue := reflect.New(value.Type()).Elem()\n   608→\t\t\t\tnewValue.Set(value)\n   609→\n   610→\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, newValue, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   611→\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n   612→\t\t\t\t}\n   613→\n   614→\t\t\t\t// Set the modified value back into the map\n   615→\t\t\t\tv.SetMapIndex(key, newValue)\n   616→\t\t\t} else {\n   617→\t\t\t\tif err := walkConfigForSecrets(ctx, value, secretsManager); err != nil {\n   618→\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n   619→\t\t\t\t}\n   620→\t\t\t}\n   621→\t\t}\n   622→\t}\n   623→\n   624→\treturn nil\n   625→}\n   626→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, This is the only explicit configuration option for tracing at the config level.\n\n### 2. GetExporter Implementation\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/traces/tracing.go`\n\n```go\nfunc GetExporter(ctx context.Context) (tracesdk.SpanExporter, func(context.Context) error, error) {\n\ttraceExpOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = autoexport.NewSpanExporter(ctx)\n\t\tif traceExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttraceExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\treturn traceExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\treturn traceExp, traceExpFunc, traceExpErr\n}\n```\n\nThe GetExporter function:\n- Uses a sync.Once pattern to ensure the exporter is only created once (singleton pattern)\n- Relies entirely on OpenTelemetry's `autoexport.NewSpanExporter()` to automatically detect and configure the exporter\n- Returns the SpanExporter, a shutdown function, and any error\n- **Comments in code state \"Supports OTLP\"** - indicating OTLP is the only supported export format\n\n### 3. Tracing Initialization in gRPC Server Setup\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` (lines 179-203)\n\n```go\n// Initialize tracingProvider regardless of configuration. No extraordinary resources\n// are consumed, or goroutines initialized until a SpanProcessor is registered.\ntracingProvider := tracesdk.NewTracerProvider(\n\ttracesdk.WithResource(otelResource),\n)\n\nserver.onShutdown(tracingProvider.Shutdown)\n\nif cfg.Tracing.Enabled {\n\texp, traceExpShutdown, err := tracing.GetExporter(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating tracing exporter: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(traceExpShutdown)\n\n\ttracingProcessor := tracesdk.NewBatchSpanProcessor(exp)\n\tserver.onShutdown(tracingProcessor.Shutdown)\n\n\ttracingProvider.RegisterSpanProcessor(tracingProcessor)\n\tlogger.Debug(\"otel tracing enabled\")\n}\n\nopentelemetry.SetTracerProvider(tracingProvider)\nopentelemetry.SetTextMapPropagator(autoprop.NewTextMapPropagator())\n```\n\nKey initialization points:\n- TracerProvider is **always created** with an OTel Resource containing Flipt service name and version\n- The SpanExporter is **only initialized if `cfg.Tracing.Enabled` is true**\n- Uses `BatchSpanProcessor` to batch spans for efficient export\n- Text map propagator is set to `autoprop.NewTextMapPropagator()` (auto-detecting propagator format)\n- gRPC instrumentation is enabled conditionally (lines 401-405):\n  ```go\n  if cfg.Tracing.Enabled {\n      statsHandler := otelgrpc.NewServerHandler()\n      grpcOpts = append(grpcOpts, grpc.StatsHandler(statsHandler))\n      ipch = ipch.WithServerStatsHandler(statsHandler)\n  }\n  ```\n\n### 4. teams := range allowedTeams {\n   136→\t\t\t\tif slices.Contains(orgs, org) {\n   137→\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   138→\t\t\t\t}\n   139→\n   140→\t\t\t\tif i := slices.IndexFunc(orgs, func(o string) bool {\n   141→\t\t\t\t\treturn org == strings.ToLower(o)\n   142→\t\t\t\t}); i != -1 {\n   143→\t\t\t\t\tdelete(allowedTeams, org)\n   144→\t\t\t\t\tallowedTeams[orgs[i]] = teams\n   145→\t\t\t\t}\n   146→\n   147→\t\t\t}\n   148→\t\t\tv.Set(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_teams\", allowedTeams)\n   149→\t\t}\n   150→\t}\n   151→\n   152→\tvar (\n   153→\t\tresult      = &Result{Config: cfg}\n   154→\t\tdeprecators []deprecator\n   155→\t\tdefaulters  []defaulter\n   156→\t\tvalidators  []validator\n   157→\t)\n   158→\n   159→\tf := func(field any) {\n   160→\t\t// for-each deprecator implementing field we collect\n   161→\t\t// them up and return them to be run before unmarshalling and before setting defaults.\n   162→\t\tif deprecator, ok := field.(deprecator); ok {\n   163→\t\t\tdeprecators = append(deprecators, deprecator)\n   164→\t\t}\n   165→\n   166→\t\t// for-each defaulter implementing fields we invoke\n   167→\t\t// setting any defaults during this prepare stage\n   168→\t\t// on the supplied viper.\n   169→\t\tif defaulter, ok := field.(defaulter); ok {\n   170→\t\t\tdefaulters = append(defaulters, defaulter)\n   171→\t\t}\n   172→\n   173→\t\t// for-each validator implementing field we collect\n   174→\t\t// them up and return them to be validated after\n   175→\t\t// unmarshalling.\n   176→\t\tif validator, ok := field.(validator); ok {\n   177→\t\t\tvalidators = append(validators, validator)\n   178→\t\t}\n   179→\t}\n   180→\n   181→\t// invoke the field visitor on the root config firsts\n   182→\troot := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Interface()\n   183→\tf(root)\n   184→\n   185→\t// these are reflected config top-level types for fields where\n   186→\t// they have been marked as experimental and their associated\n   187→\t// flag has enabled set to false.\n   188→\tvar skippedTypes []reflect.Type\n   189→\n   190→\tval := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem()\n   191→\tfor i := range val.NumField() {\n   192→\t\t// search for all expected env vars since Viper cannot\n   193→\t\t// infer when doing Unmarshal + AutomaticEnv.\n   194→\t\t// see: https://github.com/spf13/viper/issues/761\n   195→\t\tstructField := val.Type().Field(i)\n   196→\t\tif exp := structField.Tag.Get(\"experiment\"); exp != \"\" {\n   197→\t\t\tif !v.GetBool(fmt.Sprintf(\"experimental.%s.enabled\", exp)) {\n   198→\t\t\t\tskippedTypes = append(skippedTypes, structField.Type)\n   199→\t\t\t}\n   200→\t\t}\n   201→\n   202→\t\tkey := fieldKey(structField)\n   203→\t\tbindEnvVars(v, getFliptEnvs(), []string{key}, structField.Type)\n   204→\n   205→\t\tfield := val.Field(i).Addr().Interface()\n   206→\t\tf(field)\n   207→\t}\n   208→\n   209→\t// run any deprecations checks\n   210→\tfor _, deprecator := range deprecators {\n   211→\t\twarnings := deprecator.deprecations(v)\n   212→\t\tfor _, warning := range warnings {\n   213→\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, warning.Message())\n   214→\t\t}\n   215→\t}\n   216→\n   217→\t// run any defaulters\n   218→\tfor _, defaulter := range defaulters {\n   219→\t\tif err := defaulter.setDefaults(v); err != nil {\n   220→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   221→\t\t}\n   222→\t}\n   223→\n   224→\tif err := v.Unmarshal(cfg, viper.DecodeHook(\n   225→\t\tmapstructure.ComposeDecodeHookFunc(\n   226→\t\t\tappend(DecodeHooks, experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(skippedTypes...))...,\n   227→\t\t),\n   228→\t)); err != nil {\n   229→\t\treturn nil, err\n   230→\t}\n   231→\n   232→\t// run any validation steps\n   233→\tfor _, validator := range validators {\n   234→\t\tif err := validator.validate(); err != nil {\n   235→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   236→\t\t}\n   237→\t}\n   238→\n   239→\treturn result, nil\n   240→}\n   241→\n   242→// getConfigFile provides a file from different types of storage.\n   243→func getConfigFile(ctx context.Context, path string) (fs.File, error) {\n   244→\tu, err := url.Parse(path)\n   245→\tif err != nil {\n   246→\t\treturn nil, err\n   247→\t}\n   248→\n   249→\tif slices.Contains(object.SupportedSchemes(), u.Scheme) {\n   250→\t\tkey := strings.TrimPrefix(u.Path, \"/\")\n   251→\t\tu.Path = \"\"\n   252→\t\tbucket, err := object.OpenBucket(ctx, u)\n   253→\t\tif err != nil {\n   254→\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   255→\t\t}\n   256→\t\tdefer bucket.Close()\n   257→\t\tbucket.SetIOFSCallback(func() (context.Context, *blob.ReaderOptions) { return ctx, nil })\n   258→\t\treturn bucket.Open(key)\n   259→\t}\n   260→\n   261→\t// assumes that the local file is used\n   262→\tfile, err := os.Open(path)\n   263→\tif err != nil {\n   264→\t\treturn nil, err\n   265→\t}\n   266→\treturn file, nil\n   267→}\n   268→\n   269→type defaulter interface {\n   270→\tsetDefaults(v *viper.Viper) error\n   271→}\n   272→\n   273→type validator interface {\n   274→\tvalidate() error\n   275→}\n   276→\n   277→type deprecator interface {\n   278→\tdeprecations(v *viper.Viper) []deprecated\n   279→}\n   280→\n   281→// fieldKey returns the name to be used when deriving a fields env var key.\n   282→// If marked as squash the key will be the empty string.\n   283→// Otherwise, it is derived from the lowercase name of the field.\n   284→func fieldKey(field reflect.StructField) string {\n   285→\tif tag := field.Tag.Get(\"mapstructure\"); tag != \"\" {\n   286→\t\ttag, attr, ok := strings.Cut(tag, \",\")\n   287→\t\tif !ok || attr == \"squash\" || attr == \"omitempty\" {\n   288→\t\t\treturn tag\n   289→\t\t}\n   290→\t}\n   291→\n   292→\treturn strings.ToLower(field.Name)\n   293→}\n   294→\n   295→type envBinder interface {\n   296→\tMustBindEnv(...string)\n   297→}\n   298→\n   299→// bindEnvVars descends into the provided struct field binding any expected\n   300→// environment variable keys it finds reflecting struct and field tags.\n   301→func bindEnvVars(v envBinder, env, prefixes []string, typ reflect.Type) {\n   302→\t// descend through pointers\n   303→\tif typ.Kind() == reflect.Pointer {\n   304→\t\ttyp = typ.Elem()\n   305→\t}\n   306→\n   307→\tswitch typ.Kind() {\n   308→\tcase reflect.Map:\n   309→\t\t// recurse into bindEnvVars while signifying that the last\n   310→\t\t// key was unbound using the wildcard \"*\".\n   311→\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, append(prefixes, wildcard), typ.Elem())\n   312→\n   313→\t\treturn\n   314→\tcase reflect.Struct, reflect.Slice:\n   315→\t\tif typ.Kind() == reflect.Slice {\n   316→\t\t\tif typ = typ.Elem(); typ.Kind() != reflect.Struct {\n   317→\t\t\t\tbreak\n   318→\t\t\t}\n   319→\t\t}\n   320→\n   321→\t\tfor structField := range typ.Fields() {\n   322→\t\t\tkey := fieldKey(structField)\n   323→\n   324→\t\t\tbind(env, prefixes, key, func(prefixes []string) {\n   325→\t\t\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, prefixes, structField.Type)\n   326→\t\t\t})\n   327→\t\t}\n   328→\n   329→\t\treturn\n   330→\t}\n   331→\n   332→\tbind(env, prefixes, \"\", func(prefixes []string) {\n   333→\t\tv.MustBindEnv(strings.Join(prefixes, \".\"))\n   334→\t})\n   335→}\n   336→\n   337→const wildcard = \"*\"\n   338→\n   339→func appendIfNotEmpty(s []string, v ...string) []string {\n   340→\tfor _, vs := range v {\n   341→\t\tif vs != \"\" {\n   342→\t\t\ts = append(s, vs)\n   343→\t\t}\n   344→\t}\n   345→\n   346→\treturn s\n   347→}\n   348→\n   349→// bind invokes the supplied function \"fn\" with each possible set of\n   350→// prefixes for the next prefix (\"next\").\n   351→// If the last prefix is \"*\" then we must search the current environment\n   352→// for matching env vars to obtain the potential keys which populate\n   353→// the unbound map keys.\n   354→func bind(env, prefixes []string, next string, fn func([]string)) {\n   355→\t// given the previous entry is non-existent or not the wildcard\n   356→\tif len(prefixes) < 1 || prefixes[len(prefixes)-1] != wildcard {\n   357→\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(prefixes, next))\n   358→\t\treturn\n   359→\t}\n   360→\n   361→\t// drop the wildcard and derive all the possible keys from\n   362→\t// existing environment variables.\n   363→\tp := make([]string, len(prefixes)-1)\n   364→\tcopy(p, prefixes[:len(prefixes)-1])\n   365→\n   366→\tvar (\n   367→\t\t// makezero linter doesn't take note of subsequent copy\n   368→\t\t// nolint https://github.com/ashanbrown/makezero/issues/12\n   369→\t\tprefix = strings.ToUpper(strings.Join(append(p, \"\"), \"_\"))\n   370→\t\tkeys   = strippedKeys(env, prefix, strings.ToUpper(next))\n   371→\t)\n   372→\n   373→\tfor _, key := range keys {\n   374→\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(p, strings.ToLower(key), next))\n   375→\t}\n   376→}\n   377→\n   378→// strippedKeys returns a set of keys derived from a list of env var keys.\n   379→// It starts by filtering and stripping each key with a matching prefix.\n   380→// Given a child delimiter string is supplied it also trims the delimeter string\n   381→// and any remaining characters after this suffix.\n   382→//\n   383→// e.g strippedKeys([\"A_B_C_D\", \"A_B_F_D\", \"A_B_E_D_G\"], \"A_B\", \"D\")\n   384→// returns [\"c\", \"f\", \"e\"]\n   385→//\n   386→// It's purpose is to extract the parts of env vars which are likely\n   387→// keys in an arbitrary map type.\n   388→func strippedKeys(envs []string, prefix, delim string) (keys []string) {\n   389→\tfor _, env := range envs {\n   390→\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, prefix) {\n   391→\t\t\tenv = env[len(prefix):]\n   392→\t\t\tif env == \"\" {\n   393→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   394→\t\t\t}\n   395→\n   396→\t\t\tif delim == \"\" {\n   397→\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, env)\n   398→\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   399→\t\t\t}\n   400→\n   401→\t\t\t// cut the string on the child key and take the left hand component\n   402→\t\t\tif left, _, ok := strings.Cut(env, \"_\"+delim); ok {\n   403→\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, left)\n   404→\t\t\t}\n   405→\t\t}\n   406→\t}\n   407→\treturn\n   408→}\n   409→\n   410→// getFliptEnvs returns all environment variables which have FLIPT_\n   411→// as a prefix. It also strips this prefix before appending them to the\n   412→// resulting set.\n   413→func getFliptEnvs() (envs []string) {\n   414→\tconst prefix = \"FLIPT_\"\n   415→\tfor _, e := range os.Environ() {\n   416→\t\tkey, _, ok := strings.Cut(e, \"=\")\n   417→\t\tif ok && strings.HasPrefix(key, prefix) {\n   418→\t\t\t// strip FLIPT_ off env vars for convenience\n   419→\t\t\tenvs = append(envs, key[len(prefix):])\n   420→\t\t}\n   421→\t}\n   422→\treturn envs\n   423→}\n   424→\n   425→func (c *Config) validate() error {\n   426→\tif c.Version != \"\" {\n   427→\t\tif 453→}\n   454→\n   455→func (c *Config) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n   456→\tvar (\n   457→\t\tout []byte\n   458→\t\terr error\n   459→\t)\n   460→\n   461→\tif 529→\t\tt reflect.Type,\n   530→\t\tdata any,\n   531→\t) (any, error) {\n   532→\t\tif f.Kind() != reflect.String || f != reflect.TypeFor[string]() {\n   533→\t\t\treturn data, nil\n   534→\t\t}\n Let me compile a comprehensive analysis document:\n\n## Detailed Summary: Flipt Metrics Exporters Pattern\n\nBased on my exploration of the Flipt codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of how metrics exporters are configured and implemented. This serves as the template for implementing tracing exporters.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Configuration Structure (Type-Safe with Enum/Constants)\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/config/metrics.go`\n\n```go\ntype MetricsExporter string\n\nconst (\n\tMetricsPrometheus MetricsExporter = \"prometheus\"\n\tMetricsOTLP       MetricsExporter = \"otlp\"\n)\n\ntype MetricsConfig struct {\n\tEnabled  bool            `json:\"enabled\" mapstructure:\"enabled\" yaml:\"enabled\"`\n\tExporter MetricsExporter `json:\"exporter,omitempty\" mapstructure:\"exporter\" yaml:\"exporter,omitempty\"`\n}\n\nfunc (c *MetricsConfig) setDefaults(v *viper.Viper) error {\n\tv.SetDefault(\"metrics.enabled\", true)\n\tv.SetDefault(\"metrics.exporter\", MetricsPrometheus)\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n**Key Pattern Elements:**\n- Defines a string type alias `MetricsExporter` for type safety\n- Uses const with the type alias to define allowed values\n- Implements the `defaulter` interface with `setDefaults()` method\n- YAML/JSON serialization with `mapstructure` tags for config file support\n- Viper integration for defaults and environment variable override support\n\n---\n\n### 2. Exporter Selection Logic with Switch Statement\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/metrics/metrics.go`\n\n```go\nfunc GetExporter(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.MetricsConfig) (sdkmetric.Reader, func(context.Context) error, error) {\n\tmetricExpOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\tswitch cfg.Exporter {\n\t\tcase config.MetricsPrometheus:\n\t\t\t// exporter registers itself on the prom client DefaultRegistrar\n\t\t\tmetricExp, metricExpErr = prometheus.New()\n\t\t\tif metricExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase config.MetricsOTLP:\n\t\t\tmetricExp, metricExpErr = autoexport.NewMetricReader(ctx)\n\t\t\tif metricExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tmetricExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\t\treturn metricExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tmetricExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported metrics exporter: %s\", cfg.Exporter)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\treturn metricExp, metricExpFunc, metricExpErr\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern Key Features:**\n- **Singleton Pattern:** Uses `sync.Once` to ensure exporter is created only once\n  ```go\n  var (\n      metricExpOnce sync.Once\n      metricExp     sdkmetric.Reader\n      metricExpFunc func(context.Context) error = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n      metricExpErr  error\n  )\n  ```\n- **Switch Statement:** Handles each exporter type separately\n- **Type-Safe Cases:** Uses named constants from config package (`config.MetricsPrometheus`, `config.MetricsOTLP`)\n- **Shutdown Function:** Returns optional shutdown function (defaults to no-op)\n- **Error Handling:** Default case captures unsupported exporters with formatted error message\n\n---\n\n### 3. Error Handling for Unsupported Exporters\n\nThe pattern uses a default case in the switch statement:\n```go\ndefault:\n\tmetricExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported metrics exporter: %s\", cfg.Exporter)\n\treturn\n```\n\nThis ensures:\n- Type-safe string that can't be arbitrary values\n- Clear, descriptive error messages\n- Compile-time checking through const values in switch cases\n\n---\n\n### 4. Schema Definition with Allowed Values\n\n**File:** `/workspace/config/flipt.schema.cue`\n\n```cue\n#metrics: {\n\tenabled?:  bool | *true\n\texporter?: *\"prometheus\" | \"otlp\"\n}\n```\n\n**Schema Pattern Features:**\n- Uses CUE's union type syntax to enforce allowed values: `*\"prometheus\" | \"otlp\"`\n- Asterisk `*` denotes the default value\n- Optional fields marked with `?`\n- Integrates with JSON Schema generation for documentation and validation\n\n---\n\n### 5. Default Configuration Values\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/config/config.go` (Default() function)\n\n```go\nMetrics: MetricsConfig{\n\tEnabled:  true,\n\tExporter: MetricsPrometheus,\n},\n```\n\n**Additionally in setDefaults():**\n```go\nv.SetDefault(\"metrics.enabled\", true)\nv.SetDefault(\"metrics.exporter\", MetricsPrometheus)\n```\n\n**Default Strategy:**\n- Two-level defaults: constructor defaults + setDefaults() viper defaults\n- Prometheus is the default exporter (safe choice, no external dependencies)\n- Metrics enabled by default (true)\n\n---\n\n### 6. How GetExporter is Called\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` (lines 152-177)\n\n```go\nif cfg.Metrics.Enabled {\n\tmetricExp, metricExpShutdown, err := metrics.GetExporter(ctx, &cfg.Metrics)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating metrics exporter: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(metricExpShutdown)\n\n\tmeterProvider := metricsdk.NewMeterProvider(\n\t\tmetricsdk.WithResource(otelResource),\n\t\tmetricsdk.WithReader(metricExp),\n\t)\n\topentelemetry.SetMeterProvider(meterProvider)\n\tserver.onShutdown(meterProvider.Shutdown)\n\n\t// We only want to start the runtime metrics by open telemetry if the user have chosen\n\t// to use OTLP because the Prometheus endpoint already exposes those metrics.\n\tif cfg.Metrics.Exporter == config.MetricsOTLP {\n\t\terr = otlpruntime.Start(otlpruntime.WithMeterProvider(meterProvider))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"starting runtime metric exporter: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.Debug(\"otel metrics enabled\", zap.String(\"exporter\", string(cfg.Metrics.Exporter)))\n}\n```\n\n**Integration Pattern:**\n- Conditional initialization based on `cfg.Metrics.Enabled`\n- Error handling with context wrapper\n- Shutdown function registration in stack (LIFO)\n- Type-safe exporter comparison for conditional logic\n- Logging with string conversion of exporter type\n\n---\n\n### 7. Testing Pattern\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/metrics/metrics_test.go`\n\n```go\nfunc TestGetExporter(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname    string\n\t\tcfg     *config.MetricsConfig\n\t\twantErr error\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"prometheus exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: config.MetricsPrometheus,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"otlp exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: config.MetricsOTLP,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"unsupported exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: \"unsupported\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: errors.New(\"unsupported metrics exporter: unsupported\"),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset the singleton state for each test\n\t\t\tmetricExpOnce = sync.Once{}\n\t\t\tmetricExp = nil\n\t\t\tmetricExpFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n\t\t\tmetricExpErr = nil\n\n\t\t\texp, expFunc, err := GetExporter(t.Context(), tt.cfg)\n\t\t\tif tt.wantErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\trequire.EqualError(t, err, tt.wantErr.Error())\n\t\t\t\tassert.Nil(t, exp, \"expected nil exporter when error occurs\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\tassert.NotNil(t, exp)\n\t\t\tassert.NotNil(t, expFunc)\n\n\t\t\tt.Cleanup(func() {\n\t\t\t\terr := expFunc(context.Background())\n\t\t\t\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Pattern Elements:**\n- **Table-Driven Tests:** Multiple cases with different configurations\n- **Singleton Reset:** Manually reset singleton state between tests (`metricExpOnce = sync.Once{}`)\n- **Error Testing:** Dedicated test case for unsupported values\n- **Cleanup:** Proper shutdown testing via cleanup functions\n- **Assertions:** Both `testify/assert` and `testify/require` used appropriately\n- **Context:** Uses `t.Context()` for test context\n\n---\n\n### Current Tracing Exporter Selection Logic with Switch Statement\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/metrics/metrics.go`\n\n```go\nfunc GetExporter(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.MetricsConfig) (sdkmetric.Reader, func(context.Context) error, error) {\n\tmetricExpOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\tswitch cfg.Exporter {\n\t\tcase config.MetricsPrometheus:\n\t\t\t// exporter registers itself on the prom client DefaultRegistrar\n\t\t\tmetricExp, metricExpErr = prometheus.New()\n\t\t\tif metricExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase config.MetricsOTLP:\n\t\t\tmetricExp, metricExpErr = autoexport.NewMetricReader(ctx)\n\t\t\tif metricExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tmetricExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\t\treturn metricExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tmetricExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported metrics exporter: %s\", cfg.Exporter)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\treturn metricExp, metricExpFunc, metricExpErr\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern Key Features:**\n- **Singleton Pattern:** Uses `sync.Once` to ensure exporter is created only once\n  ```go\n  var (\n      metricExpOnce sync.Once\n      metricExp     sdkmetric.Reader\n      metricExpFunc func(context.Context) error = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n      metricExpErr  error\n  )\n  ```\n- **Switch Statement:** Handles each exporter type separately\n- **Type-Safe Cases:** Uses named constants from config package (`config.MetricsPrometheus`, `config.MetricsOTLP`)\n- **Shutdown Function:** Returns optional shutdown function (defaults to no-op)\n- **Error Handling:** Default case captures unsupported exporters with formatted error message\n\n---\n\n### 3. Error Handling for Unsupported Exporters\n\nThe pattern uses a default case in the switch statement:\n```go\ndefault:\n\tmetricExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported metrics exporter: %s\", cfg.Exporter)\n\treturn\n```\n\nThis ensures:\n- Type-safe string that can't be arbitrary values\n- Clear, descriptive error messages\n- Compile-time checking through const values in switch cases\n\n---\n\n### 4. Schema Definition with Allowed Values\n\n**File:** `/workspace/config/flipt.schema.cue`\n\n```cue\n#metrics: {\n\tenabled?:  bool | *true\n\texporter?: *\"prometheus\" | \"otlp\"\n}\n```\n\n**Schema Pattern Features:**\n- Uses CUE's union type syntax to enforce allowed values: `*\"prometheus\" | \"otlp\"`\n- Asterisk `*` denotes the default value\n- Optional fields marked with `?`\n- Integrates with JSON Schema generation for documentation and validation\n\n---\n\n### 5. Default Configuration Values\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/config/config.go` (Default() function)\n\n```go\nMetrics: MetricsConfig{\n\tEnabled:  true,\n\tExporter: MetricsPrometheus,\n},\n```\n\n**Additionally in setDefaults():**\n```go\nv.SetDefault(\"metrics.enabled\", true)\nv.SetDefault(\"metrics.exporter\", MetricsPrometheus)\n```\n\n**Default Strategy:**\n- Two-level defaults: constructor defaults + setDefaults() viper defaults\n- Prometheus is the default exporter (safe choice, no external dependencies)\n- Metrics enabled by default (true)\n\n---\n\n### 6. How GetExporter is Called\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/cmd/grpc.go` (lines 152-177)\n\n```go\nif cfg.Metrics.Enabled {\n\tmetricExp, metricExpShutdown, err := metrics.GetExporter(ctx, &cfg.Metrics)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating metrics exporter: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tserver.onShutdown(metricExpShutdown)\n\n\tmeterProvider := metricsdk.NewMeterProvider(\n\t\tmetricsdk.WithResource(otelResource),\n\t\tmetricsdk.WithReader(metricExp),\n\t)\n\topentelemetry.SetMeterProvider(meterProvider)\n\tserver.onShutdown(meterProvider.Shutdown)\n\n\t// We only want to start the runtime metrics by open telemetry if the user have chosen\n\t// to use OTLP because the Prometheus endpoint already exposes those metrics.\n\tif cfg.Metrics.Exporter == config.MetricsOTLP {\n\t\terr = otlpruntime.Start(otlpruntime.WithMeterProvider(meterProvider))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"starting runtime metric exporter: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tlogger.Debug(\"otel metrics enabled\", zap.String(\"exporter\", string(cfg.Metrics.Exporter)))\n}\n```\n\n**Integration Pattern:**\n- Conditional initialization based on `cfg.Metrics.Enabled`\n- Error handling with context wrapper\n- Shutdown function registration in stack (LIFO)\n- Type-safe exporter comparison for conditional logic\n- Logging with string conversion of exporter type\n\n---\n\n### 7. Testing Pattern\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/metrics/metrics_test.go`\n\n```go\nfunc TestGetExporter(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname    string\n\t\tcfg     *config.MetricsConfig\n\t\twantErr error\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"prometheus exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: config.MetricsPrometheus,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"otlp exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: config.MetricsOTLP,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"unsupported exporter\",\n\t\t\tcfg: &config.MetricsConfig{\n\t\t\t\tExporter: \"unsupported\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantErr: errors.New(\"unsupported metrics exporter: unsupported\"),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset the singleton state for each test\n\t\t\tmetricExpOnce = sync.Once{}\n\t\t\tmetricExp = nil\n\t\t\tmetricExpFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n\t\t\tmetricExpErr = nil\n\n\t\t\texp, expFunc, err := GetExporter(t.Context(), tt.cfg)\n\t\t\tif tt.wantErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\trequire.EqualError(t, err, tt.wantErr.Error())\n\t\t\t\tassert.Nil(t, exp, \"expected nil exporter when error occurs\")\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\tassert.NotNil(t, exp)\n\t\t\tassert.NotNil(t, expFunc)\n\n\t\t\tt.Cleanup(func() {\n\t\t\t\terr := expFunc(context.Background())\n\t\t\t\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n```\n\n**Testing Pattern Elements:**\n- **Table-Driven Tests:** Multiple cases with different configurations\n- **Singleton Reset:** Manually reset singleton state between tests (`metricExpOnce = sync.Once{}`)\n- **Error Testing:** Dedicated test case for unsupported values\n- **Cleanup:** Proper shutdown testing via cleanup functions\n- **Assertions:** Both `testify/assert` and `testify/require` used appropriately\n- **Context:** Uses `t.Context()` for test context\n\n---\n\n### Current Tracing Implementation (For Comparison)\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/config/tracing.go`\n\n```go\ntype TracingConfig struct {\n\tEnabled bool `json:\"enabled\" mapstructure:\"enabled\" yaml:\"enabled\"`\n}\n\nfunc (c *TracingConfig) setDefaults(v *viper.Viper) error {\n\tv.SetDefault(\"tracing.enabled\", false)\n\treturn nil\n}\n```\n\n**File:** `/workspace/internal/otel/traces/tracing.go`\n\n```go\nfunc GetExporter(ctx context.Context) (tracesdk.SpanExporter, func(context.Context) error, error) {\n\ttraceExpOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = autoexport.NewSpanExporter(ctx)\n\t\tif traceExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttraceExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\treturn traceExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\treturn traceExp, traceExpFunc, traceExpErr\n}\n```\n\n**Current Limitation:** Tracing currently only uses OTLP via autoexport. It lacks the exporter selection pattern that metrics implements.\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Pattern for Tracing Implementation\n\nTo implement tracing exporters following the metrics pattern, you would need to:\n\n1. **Extend TracingConfig** with an exporter field (string alias type with constants)\n2. **Update CUE schema** with allowed tracing exporter values\n3. **Enhance GetExporter()** with switch statement for different exporter types\n4. **Add exporter-specific logic** in grpc.go similar to metrics (conditional runtime setup)\n5. **Create comprehensive tests** with table-driven approach including error cases\n6. **Update default values** in both setDefaults() and Default() function\n\nThis is a clean, extensible, and type-safe pattern that prevents configuration errors at compile time through the use of string type aliases and const values."}],"totalDurationMs":30699,"totalTokens":41332,"totalToolUseCount":11,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":432,"cache_read_input_tokens":38141,"output_tokens":2756,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":432,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

blocks until Shutdown is called.\n   443→func (s *GRPCServer) Run() error {\n   444→\ts.logger.Info(\"starting grpc server\", zap.String(\"address\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", s.cfg.Server.Host, s.cfg.Server.GRPCPort)))\n   445→\treturn s.Serve(s.ln)\n   446→}\n   447→\n   448→// Shutdown tearsdown the entire gRPC stack including dependencies.\n   449→func (s *GRPCServer) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {\n   450→\ts.logger.Info(\"shutting down GRPC server...\")\n   451→\n   452→\t// call in reverse order to emulate pop semantics of a stack\n   453→\tfor i := len(s.shutdownFuncs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   454→\t\tif fn := s.shutdownFuncs[i]; fn != nil {\n   455→\t\t\tif err := fn(ctx); err != nil {\n   456→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   457→\t\t\t}\n   458→\t\t}\n   459→\t}\n   460→\n   461→\treturn nil\n   462→}\n   463→\n   464→type errFunc func(context.Context) error\n   465→\n   466→func (s *GRPCServer) onShutdown(fn errFunc) {\n   467→\ts.shutdownFuncs = append(s.shutdownFuncs, fn)\n   468→}\n   469→\n   470→var (\n   471→\tauthzOnce sync.Once\n   472→\tvalidator authz.Verifier\n   473→\tauthzFunc errFunc = func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n   474→\tauthzErr  error\n   475→)\n   476→\n   477→func getAuthz(ctx context.Context, logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config) (authz.Verifier, errFunc, error) {\n   478→\tauthzOnce.Do(func() {\n   479→\t\tvar err error\n   480→\t\tvalidator, err = authzrego.NewEngine(ctx, logger, cfg)\n   481→\t\tif err != nil {\n   482→\t\t\tauthzErr = fmt.Errorf(\"creating authorization policy engine: %w\", err)\n   483→\t\t\treturn\n   484→\t\t}\n   485→\t})\n   486→\n   487→\treturn validator, authzFunc, authzErr\n   488→}\n   489→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider *Config\n\tWarnings []string\n}\n\n// Dir returns the default root directory for Flipt configuration\nfunc Dir() (string, error) {\n\tconfigDir, err := os.UserConfigDir()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"getting user config dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn filepath.Join(configDir, \"flipt\"), nil\n}\n\nfunc Load(ctx context.Context, path string) (*Result, error) {\n\tv := viper.New()\n\tv.SetEnvPrefix(EnvPrefix)\n\tv.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(\".\", \"_\"))\n\tv.AutomaticEnv()\n\n\tvar cfg *Config\n\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\tcfg = Default()\n\t} else {\n\t\tcfg = &Config{}\n\t\tfile, err := getConfigFile(ctx, path)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer file.Close()\n\t\tstat, err := file.Stat()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// reimplement logic from v.ReadInConfig()\n\t\tv.SetConfigFile(stat.Name())\n\t\text := filepath.Ext(stat.Name())\n\t\tif len(ext) > 1 {\n\t\t\text = ext[1:]\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !slices.Contains(viper.SupportedExts, ext) {\n\t\t\treturn nil, viper.UnsupportedConfigError(ext)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := v.ReadConfig(file); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// This is a fix for issue #4428. By default, Viper uses lowercase keys and the allowed_teams with case-sensitive keys\n\t\t// will be converted to lowercase. This will cause issues when matching against allowed_organizations. This workaround\n\t\t// tries to restore the original case of the keys in allowed_teams if they match any of the allowed_organizations.\n\t\tif v.IsSet(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_teams\") && v.IsSet(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_organizations\") {\n\t\t\tallowedTeams := v.GetStringMapStringSlice(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_teams\")\n\t\t\torgs := (v.GetStringSlice(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_organizations\"))\n\t\t\tfor org, teams := range allowedTeams {\n\t\t\t\tif slices.Contains(orgs, org) {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif i := slices.IndexFunc(orgs, func(o string) bool {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn org == strings.ToLower(o)\n\t\t\t\t}); i != -1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tdelete(allowedTeams, org)\n\t\t\t\t\tallowedTeams[orgs[i]] = teams\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tv.Set(\"authentication.methods.github.allowed_teams\", allowedTeams)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tresult      = &Result{Config: cfg}\n\t\tdeprecators []deprecator\n\t\tdefaulters  []defaulter\n\t\tvalidators  []validator\n\t)\n\n\tf := func(field any) {\n\t\t// for-each deprecator implementing field we collect\n\t\t// them up and return them to be run before unmarshalling and before setting defaults.\n\t\tif deprecator, ok := field.(deprecator); ok {\n\t\t\tdeprecators = append(deprecators, deprecator)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// for-each defaulter implementing fields we invoke\n\t\t// setting any defaults during this prepare stage\n\t\t// on the supplied viper.\n\t\tif defaulter, ok := field.(defaulter); ok {\n\t\t\tdefaulters = append(defaulters, defaulter)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// for-each validator implementing field we collect\n\t\t// them up and return them to be validated after\n\t\t// unmarshalling.\n\t\tif validator, ok := field.(validator); ok {\n\t\t\tvalidators = append(validators, validator)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// invoke the field visitor on the root config firsts\n\troot := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Interface()\n\tf(root)\n\n\t// these are reflected config top-level types for fields where\n\t// they have been marked as experimental and their associated\n\t// flag has enabled set to false.\n\tvar skippedTypes []reflect.Type\n\n\tval := reflect.ValueOf(cfg).Elem()\n\tfor i := range val.NumField() {\n\t\t// search for all expected env vars since Viper cannot\n\t\t// infer when doing Unmarshal + AutomaticEnv.\n\t\t// see: https://github.com/spf13/viper/issues/761\n\t\tstructField := val.Type().Field(i)\n\t\tif exp := structField.Tag.Get(\"experiment\"); exp != \"\" {\n\t\t\tif !v.GetBool(fmt.Sprintf(\"experimental.%s.enabled\", exp)) {\n\t\t\t\tskippedTypes = append(skippedTypes, structField.Type)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tkey := fieldKey(structField)\n\t\tbindEnvVars(v, getFliptEnvs(), []string{key}, structField.Type)\n\n\t\tfield := val.Field(i).Addr().Interface()\n\t\tf(field)\n\t}\n\n\t// run any deprecations checks\n\tfor _, deprecator := range deprecators {\n\t\twarnings := deprecator.deprecations(v)\n\t\tfor _, warning := range warnings {\n\t\t\tresult.Warnings = append(result.Warnings, warning.Message())\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// run any defaulters\n\tfor _, defaulter := range defaulters {\n\t\tif err := defaulter.setDefaults(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif err := v.Unmarshal(cfg, viper.DecodeHook(\n\t\tmapstructure.ComposeDecodeHookFunc(\n\t\t\tappend(DecodeHooks, experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(skippedTypes...))...,\n\t\t),\n\t)); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// run any validation steps\n\tfor _, validator := range validators {\n\t\tif err := validator.validate(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// getConfigFile provides a file from different types of storage.\nfunc getConfigFile(ctx context.Context, path string) (fs.File, error) {\n\tu, err := url.Parse(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif slices.Contains(object.SupportedSchemes(), u.Scheme) {\n\t\tkey := strings.TrimPrefix(u.Path, \"/\")\n\t\tu.Path = \"\"\n\t\tbucket, err := object.OpenBucket(ctx, u)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer bucket.Close()\n\t\tbucket.SetIOFSCallback(func() (context.Context, *blob.ReaderOptions) { return ctx, nil })\n\t\treturn bucket.Open(key)\n\t}\n\n\t// assumes that the local file is used\n\tfile, err := os.Open(path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\treturn file, nil\n}\n\ntype defaulter interface {\n\tsetDefaults(v *viper.Viper) error\n}\n\ntype validator interface {\n\tvalidate() error\n}\n\ntype deprecator interface {\n\tdeprecations(v *viper.Viper) []deprecated\n}\n\n// fieldKey returns the name to be used when deriving a fields env var key.\n// If marked as squash the key will be the empty string.\n// Otherwise, it is derived from the lowercase name of the field.\nfunc fieldKey(field reflect.StructField) string {\n\tif tag := field.Tag.Get(\"mapstructure\"); tag != \"\" {\n\t\ttag, attr, ok := strings.Cut(tag, \",\")\n\t\tif !ok || attr == \"squash\" || attr == \"omitempty\" {\n\t\t\treturn tag\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.ToLower(field.Name)\n}\n\ntype envBinder interface {\n\tMustBindEnv(...string)\n}\n\n// bindEnvVars descends into the provided struct field binding any expected\n// environment variable keys it finds reflecting struct and field tags.\nfunc bindEnvVars(v envBinder, env, prefixes []string, typ reflect.Type) {\n\t// descend through pointers\n\tif typ.Kind() == reflect.Pointer {\n\t\ttyp = typ.Elem()\n\t}\n\n\tswitch typ.Kind() {\n\tcase reflect.Map:\n\t\t// recurse into bindEnvVars while signifying that the last\n\t\t// key was unbound using the wildcard \"*\".\n\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, append(prefixes, wildcard), typ.Elem())\n\n\t\treturn\n\tcase reflect.Struct, reflect.Slice:\n\t\tif typ.Kind() == reflect.Slice {\n\t\t\tif typ = typ.Elem(); typ.Kind() != reflect.Struct {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor structField := range typ.Fields() {\n\t\t\tkey := fieldKey(structField)\n\n\t\t\tbind(env, prefixes, key, func(prefixes []string) {\n\t\t\t\tbindEnvVars(v, env, prefixes, structField.Type)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tbind(env, prefixes, \"\", func(prefixes []string) {\n\t\tv.MustBindEnv(strings.Join(prefixes, \".\"))\n\t})\n}\n\nconst wildcard = \"*\"\n\nfunc appendIfNotEmpty(s []string, v ...string) []string {\n\tfor _, vs := range v {\n\t\tif vs != \"\" {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, vs)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// bind invokes the supplied function \"fn\" with each possible set of\n// prefixes for the next prefix (\"next\").\n// If the last prefix is \"*\" then we must search the current environment\n// for matching env vars to obtain the potential keys which populate\n// the unbound map keys.\nfunc bind(env, prefixes []string, next string, fn func([]string)) {\n\t// given the previous entry is non-existent or not the wildcard\n\tif len(prefixes) < 1 || prefixes[len(prefixes)-1] != wildcard {\n\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(prefixes, next))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// drop the wildcard and derive all the possible keys from\n\t// existing environment variables.\n\tp := make([]string, len(prefixes)-1)\n\tcopy(p, prefixes[:len(prefixes)-1])\n\n\tvar (\n\t\t// makezero linter doesn't take note of subsequent copy\n\t\t// nolint https://github.com/ashanbrown/makezero/issues/12\n\t\tprefix = strings.ToUpper(strings.Join(append(p, \"\"), \"_\"))\n\t\tkeys   = strippedKeys(env, prefix, strings.ToUpper(next))\n\t)\n\n\tfor _, key := range keys {\n\t\tfn(appendIfNotEmpty(p, strings.ToLower(key), next))\n\t}\n}\n\n// strippedKeys returns a set of keys derived from a list of env var keys.\n// It starts by filtering and stripping each key with a matching prefix.\n// Given a child delimiter string is supplied it also trims the delimeter string\n// and any remaining characters after this suffix.\n//\n// e.g strippedKeys([\"A_B_C_D\", \"A_B_F_D\", \"A_B_E_D_G\"], \"A_B\", \"D\")\n// returns [\"c\", \"f\", \"e\"]\n//\n// It's purpose is to extract the parts of env vars which are likely\n// keys in an arbitrary map type.\nfunc strippedKeys(envs []string, prefix, delim string) (keys []string) {\n\tfor _, env := range envs {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, prefix) {\n\t\t\tenv = env[len(prefix):]\n\t\t\tif env == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif delim == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, env)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// cut the string on the child key and take the left hand component\n\t\t\tif left, _, ok := strings.Cut(env, \"_\"+delim); ok {\n\t\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, left)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn\n}\n\n// getFliptEnvs returns all environment variables which have FLIPT_\n// as a prefix. It also strips this prefix before appending them to the\n// resulting set.\nfunc getFliptEnvs() (envs []string) {\n\tconst prefix = \"FLIPT_\"\n\tfor _, e := range os.Environ() {\n\t\tkey, _, ok := strings.Cut(e, \"=\")\n\t\tif ok && strings.HasPrefix(key, prefix) {\n\t\t\t// strip FLIPT_ off env vars for convenience\n\t\t\tenvs = append(envs, key[len(prefix):])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn envs\n}\n\nfunc (c *Config) validate() error {\n\tif c.Version != \"\" {\n\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(c.Version) != Version {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid version: %s\", c.Version)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif c.Authorization.Required && !c.Authentication.Required {\n\t\treturn errString(\"authorization\", \"requires authentication to also be required\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check that environment storage references exist in storage config\n\tfor envName, env := range c.Environments {\n\t\tif env.Storage == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif _, exists := c.Storage[env.Storage]; !exists {\n\t\t\treturn errString(\"environments\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%q references undefined storage %q\", envName, env.Storage))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate templates *http.Request) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tout []byte\n\t\terr error\n\t)\n\n\tif r.Header.Get(\"Accept\") == \"application/json+pretty\" {\n\t\tout, err = json.MarshalIndent(c, \"\", \"  \")\n\t} else {\n\t\tout, err = json.Marshal(c)\n\t}\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err = w.Write(out); err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n}\n\n// stringToEnumHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that converts strings to a target enum\nfunc stringToEnumHookFunc[T constraints.Integer](mappings map[string]T) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {\n\treturn func(\n\t\tf reflect.Type,\n\t\tt reflect.Type,\n\t\tdata any,\n\t) (any, error) {\n\t\tif f.Kind() != reflect.String {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif t != reflect.TypeOf(T(0)) {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tenum := mappings[data.(string)]\n\n\t\treturn enum, nil\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc experimentalFieldSkipHookFunc(types ...reflect.Type) mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {\n\treturn func(\n\t\tf reflect.Type,\n\t\tt reflect.Type,\n\t\tdata any,\n\t) (any, error) {\n\t\tif len(types) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif t.Kind() != reflect.Struct {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// skip any types that match a type in the provided set\n\t\tfor _, typ := range types {\n\t\t\tif t == typ {\n\t\t\t\treturn reflect.New(typ).Interface(), nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn data, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// stringToReferenceHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that handles:\n// - ${env:VAR} - environment variables\n// - ${secret:key} - secret references (marked as unresolved placeholders)\nfunc stringToReferenceHookFunc() mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {\n\treturn func(\n\t\tf reflect.Type,\n\t\tt reflect.Type,\n\t\tdata any,\n\t) (any, error) {\n\t\tif f.Kind() != reflect.String || f != reflect.TypeFor[string]() {\n\t\t\treturn data, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstr := data.(string)\n\n\t\t// Handle explicit environment references: ${env:VAR}\n\t\tif envReference.MatchString(str) {\n\t\t\tkey := envReference.ReplaceAllString(str, `$1`)\n\t\t\treturn os.Getenv(key), nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Secret references are left as-is and will be resolved post-load\n\t\treturn data, nil\n\t}\n}\n\n// stringToSliceHookFunc returns a DecodeHookFunc that converts\n// string to []string by splitting using strings.Fields().\nfunc stringToSliceHookFunc() mapstructure.DecodeHookFunc {\n\treturn func(\n\t\tf reflect.Kind,\n\t\tt reflect.Kind,\n\t\tdata any,\n\t) (any, error) config.TracingOTLP:\n\t\t\t// Configure OTLP endpoint from config\n\t\t\tif cfg.Endpoint != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tos.Setenv(\"OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT\", cfg.Endpoint)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = autoexport.NewSpanExporter(ctx)\n\t\t\tif traceExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\ttraceExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\t\treturn traceExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase config.TracingJaeger:\n\t\t\t// Configure Jaeger via environment\n\t\t\tos.Setenv(\"OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER\", \"jaeger\")\n\t\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = autoexport.NewSpanExporter(ctx)\n\t\t\tif traceExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\ttraceExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\t\treturn traceExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase config.TracingZipkin:\n\t\t\t// Configure Zipkin via environment\n\t\t\tos.Setenv(\"OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER\", \"zipkin\")\n\t\t\ttraceExp, traceExpErr = autoexport.NewSpanExporter(ctx)\n\t\t\tif traceExpErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\ttraceExpFunc = func(ctx context.Context) error {\n\t\t\t\treturn traceExp.Shutdown(ctx)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\ttraceExpErr = fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported tracing exporter: %s\", cfg.Exporter)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\treturn traceExp, traceExpFunc, traceExpErr\n}\n","numLines":72,"startLine":1,"totalLines":72}}}
